metaTitle: Export Table as PNG or JPEG Image | Tablesmit
metaDescription: Export your table as a high-resolution PNG or JPEG image. Embed in presentations, documents, or web pages. No browser chrome — just the table. Free, no signup.
heroHeadline: Your table as a crisp, clean image — ready to embed anywhere.
heroSubtext: Export to PNG for lossless quality or JPEG for smaller file size. Both render the table only — no toolbar, no sidebars, no browser interface. Paste it anywhere images are accepted.

benefits.heading: High resolution by default
benefits.body: Tablesmit renders at 2x pixel density before exporting. The image is crisp on retina displays and scales cleanly without blurring.

benefits.heading: Table only — perfectly cropped
benefits.body: The export captures only the table and its caption. No navbar, no sidebar, no white space around the browser window. Clean, tight, ready to use.

benefits.heading: PNG for documents and design
benefits.body: PNG is lossless — no compression artefacts. Use it for Word documents, Google Docs, presentation slides, or anywhere text legibility matters.

benefits.heading: JPEG for web and email
benefits.body: JPEG compresses to a smaller file size. Use it for web pages, email attachments, or any context where file size matters more than perfect sharpness.

steps.heading: Build and style your table
steps.body: Set headers, fill in data, apply a theme, and resize columns. The image captures exactly what you see — style it before exporting.

steps.heading: Click PNG or JPEG in the Export panel
steps.body: Find the Export section in the right sidebar. Click PNG for lossless quality or JPEG for a smaller file. The export processes in your browser.

steps.heading: The image downloads instantly
steps.body: No server upload, no wait. The image file downloads immediately, named after your table caption if one is set.

useCases: Embedding a table as a PNG image in a PowerPoint or Keynote presentation
Adding a table image to a Word document where an editable table is not needed
Exporting a JPEG for a web page or blog post where file size is a concern
Sending a table as an image attachment in an email without attaching a PDF

comparison.heading: PNG vs JPEG — which should you use?

rows.jpeg: Compressed — minor quality loss at high compression
Slightly softer at low quality settings

_codex_notes.note: comparison is an additional field for this page. Render as a clean two-column comparison table between the steps and use cases sections. This is the one feature page where a table inside the page makes complete sense — use Tablesmit's own table aesthetic to render it.
